_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices set to 100 in 2015, now stands at 141, reflecting a 41% increase since 2015. This rise indicates that the cost of these staples has climbed sharply over recent years, though the increase has been uneven across the different commodities. The data represent global commodity prices and capture fluctuations in soft commodities, which are agricultural products prone to supply shocks.
notes.movers: Coffee has seen the largest increase at 99% since 2015, followed by orange juice up 34%, wheat up 19%, and sugar up 13%. These changes are largely driven by specific weather events and crop diseases—for example, citrus greening and hurricanes have affected orange juice supplies, while frost and drought in arabica coffee regions have contributed to coffee price spikes. Such soft commodity markets tend to be thin, meaning relatively small supply disruptions can cause significant price volatility, rather than reflecting broad-based inflation pressures.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is primarily due to weather-exposed crops like coffee and orange juice, which have experienced sharp supply constraints. Given the nature of agricultural production and susceptibility to environmental factors, elevated prices may persist for several years as markets adjust and recover from these shocks.
